# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

request.stdin:
SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, category_id, json_extract(properties,'$.sealant_type') AS sealant_type, json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family
FROM products
WHERE brand = 'Sika'
  AND model = 'Sika 28T-UV8'
  AND series LIKE '%Sika Professional%'
LIMIT 20;

SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand = 'Helios' AND series LIKE '%Quick Dry Mobihel%' AND model = '1VV-8Y1' AND name = 'Wall Paint' AND json_extract(properties, '$.color_family') = 'Blue' AND json_extract(properties, '$.finish') = 'matte' AND (json_extract(properties, '$.volume_ml') = 10000 OR json_extract(properties, '$.volume_ml') = '10000' OR json_extract(properties, '$.volume') = '10000 ml') LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, category_id, json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter FROM products WHERE brand='Heco' AND series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated TopFix GTU-YPJ Wood and Drywall Screw%' AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='3 mm' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family, json_extract(properties,'$.finish') AS finish, json_extract(properties,'$.volume_ml') AS volume_ml, json_extract(properties,'$.volume') AS volume FROM products WHERE brand='Helios' AND model='1VV-8Y1' AND name='Wall Paint' LIMIT 20;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.sealant_type') AS sealant_type, json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family
FROM products
WHERE brand='Sika' AND (
  series LIKE '%Sika Professional%'
  OR model LIKE '%28T-UV8%'
  OR name LIKE '%Sealant%'
)
LIMIT 50;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool
Requested kind_id: hammers_measuring_cutting

For today's report, use the normal catalogue count: count every product record
whose kind_id matches the requested product kind. Count each SKU once. Do not
apply inventory, display, merchandising, or family-hold filters unless another
current update explicitly names them.

Requested product kind: Workshop Saw and Cutter
Requested kind_id: saws_cutters

For today's count report, count only catalogue SKUs for the requested product
kind that have at least one current inventory row in an open PowerTool store in
Vienna with available_today greater than 0. Count each SKU once even if it is
available in multiple Vienna branches.

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM products WHERE kind_id='hammers_measuring_cutting';
SELECT path FROM products WHERE kind_id='hammers_measuring_cutting' ORDER BY sku;

SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory','stores');

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"

request.stdin:
SELECT DISTINCT p.path
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'saws_cutters'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0
ORDER BY p.path;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id=i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id='drain_traps_siphons' AND s.city='Innsbruck' AND s.is_open=1 AND i.available_today>0;
SELECT DISTINCT p.path
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id=i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id='drain_traps_siphons' AND s.city='Innsbruck' AND s.is_open=1 AND i.available_today>0
ORDER BY p.path;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id=i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id='drain_traps_siphons' AND s.city='Innsbruck' AND s.is_open=1 AND i.available_today>0;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

SELECT id, path, name, city FROM stores WHERE city LIKE '%Salzburg%';
SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, properties FROM products WHERE (brand='Rawlplug' AND model='3FA-C1B') OR (brand='Uvex' AND model='Y59-F8N') OR (brand='Bernardo' AND model='3OB-CQO') OR (brand='Bostik' AND model='3S9-FF7') OR (brand='Wavin' AND model='2HC-AJD') OR (brand='Bosch' AND model='3JW-ULH');

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, path, name FROM stores
  WHERE city='Innsbruck' AND name LIKE '%Wilten%'
  LIMIT 5
), targets AS (
  SELECT 'p1' AS pid, sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco'
    AND series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='10 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p2' AS pid, sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Festool'
    AND series='Festool Stackable'
    AND model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND name='Tool Box and Bag'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p3' AS pid, sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Festool'
    AND series='Festool Stackable'
    AND model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND name='Tool Box and Bag'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='parts case'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p4' AS pid, sku, path FROM products
  WHERE brand='Heco'
    AND series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND model='TopFix GTU-YPJ'
    AND name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='3 mm'
)
SELECT ts.id AS store_id, ts.path AS store_path, ts.name AS store_name,
       t.pid, t.sku, t.path AS product_path, i.available_today
FROM target_store ts
JOIN targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=ts.id AND i.sku=t.sku
ORDER BY t.pid;

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores WHERE city='Graz' AND district='Lend'
), products_to_check AS (
  SELECT 'Ryobi planer' AS label, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ryobi'
    AND p.category='Cordless Saw and Sander'
    AND p.series='Ryobi Precision'
    AND p.model='RLM 2KQ-R4C'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='planer'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Bosch 125mm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bosch'
    AND p.category='Corded Angle Grinder'
    AND p.series='Bosch Compact'
    AND p.model='PKS 375-EOK'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.disc_diameter')='125 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Sika hybrid gray 280ml', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Sika'
    AND p.category='Sealant'
    AND p.series='Sika Professional'
    AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.sealant_type')='hybrid sealant'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Gray'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume')='280 ml'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Bondex lacquer', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bondex'
    AND p.category='Wood Stain and Deck Oil'
    AND p.series='Bondex Garden Classic'
    AND p.model='106-TS1'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='lacquer'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Sonax 450mm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Sonax'
    AND p.category='Wiper Blade'
    AND p.series='Sonax Premium Gloss'
    AND p.model='304-ZK0'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='450 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Dresselhaus wood screw 8mm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Dresselhaus'
    AND p.category='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND p.series='Dresselhaus Outdoor Pro'
    AND p.model='1VG-P5G'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
)
SELECT t.id AS store_id, t.name AS store_name, c.label, c.sku, c.path, i.available_today
FROM target_store t
LEFT JOIN products_to_check c ON 1=1
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=t.id AND i.sku=c.sku
ORDER BY c.label;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name FROM products
WHERE name LIKE '%Nut%'
   OR name LIKE '%Bolt%'
   OR name LIKE '%Washer%'
   OR name LIKE '%Tool Box%'
   OR name LIKE '%Bag%'
   OR name LIKE '%Wood%'
   OR name LIKE '%Drywall%'
   OR brand LIKE '%Heco%'
   OR brand LIKE '%Festool%'
LIMIT 200;

request.stdin:
WITH products_to_check AS (
  SELECT 'Ryobi planer' AS label, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ryobi'
    AND p.name='Cordless Saw and Sander'
    AND p.series='Ryobi Precision'
    AND p.model='RLM 2KQ-R4C'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='planer'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Bosch 125mm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bosch'
    AND p.name='Corded Angle Grinder'
    AND p.series='Bosch Compact'
    AND p.model='PKS 375-EOK'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.disc_diameter')='125 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Sika hybrid gray 280ml', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Sika'
    AND p.name='Sealant'
    AND p.series='Sika Professional'
    AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.sealant_type')='hybrid sealant'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Gray'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume')='280 ml'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Bondex lacquer', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bondex'
    AND p.name='Wood Stain and Deck Oil'
    AND p.series='Bondex Garden Classic'
    AND p.model='106-TS1'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='lacquer'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Sonax 450mm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Sonax'
    AND p.name='Wiper Blade'
    AND p.series='Sonax Premium Gloss'
    AND p.model='304-ZK0'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='450 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Dresselhaus wood screw 8mm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Dresselhaus'
    AND p.name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND p.series='Dresselhaus Outdoor Pro'
    AND p.model='1VG-P5G'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
)
SELECT c.label, c.sku, c.path, i.available_today
FROM products_to_check c
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku=c.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
ORDER BY c.label;

SELECT sku, available_today
FROM inventory
WHERE store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
  AND sku IN ('FST-3SJKL8BF','STO-12JLHT7D','STO-2R84BSHQ','FST-1HE3ZSQ6')
ORDER BY sku;

SELECT brand, series, model, name, sku, path, properties
FROM products
WHERE (brand='Ryobi' AND model='RLM 2KQ-R4C')
   OR (brand='Bosch' AND model='PKS 375-EOK')
   OR (brand='Sika' AND model='Sika 28T-UV8')
   OR (brand='Bondex' AND model='106-TS1')
   OR (brand='Sonax' AND model='304-ZK0')
   OR (brand='Dresselhaus' AND model='1VG-P5G')
LIMIT 50;

request.stdin:
SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
WHERE i.store_id='store_graz_lend'
  AND p.sku IN (
    'PWR-293I8OUS',
    'PWR-3FM3NHV4',
    'ADH-3FVPXKII',
    'PNT-3APVSF7J',
    'AUT-3UP372ID',
    'FST-VBCWS2XQ'
  )
ORDER BY p.sku;

WITH req AS (
  SELECT 'nut_bolt_washer_10mm' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco' AND p.series='Zinc Plated' AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Nut Bolt and Washer%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=10
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'tool_box_bag_tool_bag' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Festool' AND p.series='Stackable' AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'tool_box_bag_parts_case' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Festool' AND p.series='Stackable' AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='parts case'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'wood_drywall_screw_3mm_wood' AS item, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco' AND p.series='Zinc Plated' AND p.model='TopFix GTU-YPJ'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Wood and Drywall Screw%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=3
)
SELECT r.item, r.sku, r.path, i.available_today
FROM req r
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku=r.sku AND i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
ORDER BY r.item;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

response.stdout:
name,sql
basket_lines,"CREATE TABLE basket_lines (
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (basket_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
baskets,"CREATE TABLE baskets (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	discount_percent INTEGER,
	discount_reason_code TEXT,
	discount_issuer_id TEXT,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
categories,"CREATE TABLE categories (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	department TEXT NOT NULL
)"
customers,"CREATE TABLE customers (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	home_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	home_lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
employee_roles,"CREATE TABLE employee_roles (
	employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	role TEXT NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (employee_id, role),
	FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employees(id)
)"
employees,"CREATE TABLE employees (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	title TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
families,"CREATE TABLE families (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payment_lines,"CREATE TABLE payment_lines (
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	unit_price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (payment_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payments,"CREATE TABLE payments (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_archived INTEGER NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_method_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	device_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	observed_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	observed_lon REAL NOT NULL,
	three_ds_status TEXT,
	three_ds_failure_reason TEXT,
	three_ds_attempts INTEGER,
	three_ds_max_attempts INTEGER,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
product_kinds,"CREATE TABLE product_kinds (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL
)"
product_properties,"CREATE TABLE product_properties (
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	key TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_text TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_number REAL,
	PRIMARY KEY (sku, key)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
returns,"CREATE TABLE returns (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	reason_code TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id)
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
